episodes.title: INSIDE THE RAMP (Part 2): Rodrigo’s Escape from a “Cult-Like” System
episodes.description:
Description:
In Part 2 of our ongoing discussion, Rodrigo returns to share his personal story inside The Ramp — the ministry led by Karen Wheaton.

What began as a powerful movement has, according to some former insiders, raised serious concerns.

Tonight we cover:

Rodrigo’s firsthand experience
The environment and expectations inside The Ramp
Why he ultimately chose to leave
Broader concerns being raised about leadership and accountability

This conversation is part of a larger effort to carefully examine modern ministry culture and encourage discernment.

episodes.title: THE RAMP EXPOSED: “THEY KNEW… AND DID NOTHING”
episodes.description:
Tonight, we’re joined by Rodrigo — a former insider with direct experience inside The Ramp in Hamilton, Alabama — as he responds to the ministry’s official statement regarding serious allegations involving senior leadership.

Rodrigo was there. He saw the culture. And tonight, he walks through the statement line by line — exposing what he says are attempts to minimize, deflect, and distance the leadership from what actually happened.

Key issues discussed:

The framing of the incident as something that happened “years ago”
The use of vague language like “staff member” instead of acknowledging leadership ties
The reality that the accused was not just anyone — but deeply embedded in leadership and family structure
Questions about discernment and accountability in a ministry that claims to hear from God
Allegations of a broader culture of verbal abuse, control, and intimidation
Why “prayer meetings” are being used instead of real transparency and action

Rodrigo also shares firsthand experiences from inside The Ramp, including:

Leadership behavior behind closed doors
Treatment of young people within the ministry
Patterns that he says point to something deeper than a one-time incident

This is not about rumors.

This is about testimony, documented statements, and asking the questions that leadership does not want asked.

If you’ve ever been part of The Ramp — or any ministry that uses authority to silence, control, or protect itself — this conversation matters.

📌 We will continue to follow this story and examine all available evidence.
